channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: ToolSense: A Diagnostic Framework for Auditing Parametric Tool Knowledge in LLMs
item.description: arXiv:2606.12451v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models deployed as agents over large tool catalogs face a critical tool-retrieval bottleneck. As embedding-based retrieval approaches rely on compact encoders that may under-capture specialized tool semantics, parametric tool retrieval...

item.title: Arbor: Tree Search as a Cognition Layer for Autonomous Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.12563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Arbor is a multi-agent framework that introduces structured tree search as a cognition layer for autonomous agents operating in large, stateful action spaces. Prior autonomous optimization systems operate on isolated targets with stateless evaluation....

item.title: Strategic Decision Support for AI Agents
item.description: arXiv:2606.12587v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Traditionally, decision support studies how humans use machine learning models to make better decisions. In modern agentic systems, this division of roles is increasingly reversed: AI agents act on behalf of users, while humans and tools becomes...

item.title: Pythagoras-Prover: Advancing Efficient Formal Proving via Augmented Lean Formalisation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12594v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Modern Lean theorem provers achieve strong performance only with substantial training and inference compute, driven in part by scarce verified proof data and the long reasoning traces of formal proof search, making both supervised fine-tuning (SFT)...

item.title: PersonaDrive: Human-Style Retrieval-Augmented VLA Agents for Closed-Loop Driving Simulation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12616v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Closed-loop driving simulators typically populate their environments with non-ego traffic agents that behave largely the same way, produced either by rule-based traffic managers or by learned models trained toward a single behavioral mode. Recent work...

item.title: Physics-informed generative AI for semiconductor manufacturing: Enforcing hard physical constraints in generative models by construction
item.description: arXiv:2606.11247v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Generative models are increasingly used to propose designs, data, and control actions for physical systems, yet many such systems are governed by hard physical constraints rather than by perceptual plausibility. Semiconductor manufacturing provides a...

item.title: Mechanical Field Networks: Structured Neural Dynamics for Multivariate Systems
item.description: arXiv:2606.11251v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Many multivariate dynamical systems are observed only through trajectories, leaving the mechanisms governing their joint dynamics hidden. Existing approaches can impose interpretable dynamics or learn flexible state transitions, yet the resulting...

item.title: Bernstein-Schur Kernels: Random Features by Sketched Modulation and Radial Randomization
item.description: arXiv:2606.11255v2 Announce Type: new Abstract: Bernstein--Schur kernels are products of a finite-feature kernel and a completely monotone shift-invariant kernel: nonstationary kernels falling between the shift-invariant and dot-product templates random features exploit, so neither Bochner sampling...

item.title: Loss Landscape Diagnosis for Gradient-Based Gray-Scott System Inversion: Disentangling the Roles of PINN Components
item.description: arXiv:2606.11258v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Gradient-based inversion of reaction-diffusion systems is typically approached via surrogate models or physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), while the most direct route, backpropagation through the PDE's structure itself, has largely been avoided....

item.title: PermDoRA -- Understanding Adapter Interference in Language Models: Limits of Parameter-Space Geometry
item.description: arXiv:2606.11262v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Access control in large language models (LLMs) requires modular mechanisms to enable domain-specific behavior without retraining or cross-domain interference. A common hypothesis is that interference during adapter composition arises from overlap in...

item.title: EDEN: A Large-Scale Corpus of Clinical Notes for Italian
item.description: arXiv:2606.12569v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present EDEN (Emergency Department Electronic Notes), a new and unique large-scale corpus of clinical notes produced in Emergency Departments of Italian hospitals. The corpus, in its current version, is composed of approximately 4 million clinical...

item.title: Helping Figures Tell their Story! Paper-Grounded Video Generation Explaining Complex Scientific Figures
item.description: arXiv:2606.12576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific figures compress complex pipelines into a single canvas, yet understanding them requires paper-grounded, step-by-step narration aligned with visual highlights a capability missing from current video generation systems and benchmarks. To...

item.title: MARD: Mirror-Augmented Reasoning Distillation for Mechanism-Level Drug-Drug Interaction Prediction
item.description: arXiv:2606.12578v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Mechanism-level drug-drug interaction (DDI) prediction requires identifying which enzyme or pharmacodynamic axis is implicated, in which direction, and with which evidence -- not merely whether two drugs interact. We introduce a reproducible...

item.title: Constrained Semantic Decompression in LLMs through Persian Proverb-Conditioned Story Generation
item.description: arXiv:2606.12599v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transforming a dense, abstract proverb into an engaging and morally faithful narrative requires deep cultural understanding and robust semantic grounding. We frame this problem as a \emph{constrained semantic decompression} task and study...

item.title: Shopping Reasoning Bench: An Expert-Authored Benchmark for Multi-Turn Conversational Shopping Assistants
item.description: arXiv:2606.12608v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conversational shopping assistants now serve hundreds of millions of customers, yet no existing benchmark jointly evaluates the open-ended multi-turn reasoning, domain expertise, and criterion-level quality that real shopping conversations demand....

item.title: Google Turns to Samsung as AI Chip Capacity Crunch Reshapes Global Supply Chains
item.description: Google Turns to Samsung as AI Chip Capacity Crunch Reshapes Global Supply Chains The AI boom is straining global chipmaking capacity to its limits, and Google'...

item.title: China Delivers 5 Million GaN RF Chips for 6G Networks in Breakthrough Commercial Deployment
item.description: China Delivers 5 Million GaN RF Chips for 6G Networks in Breakthrough Commercial Deployment China has delivered five million gallium nitride (GaN) radio freque...

item.title: AI Opens the Gates: How China's Tech Giants Are Democratizing Gaokao Guidance for 12.9 Million Students
item.description: AI Opens the Gates: How China's Tech Giants Are Democratizing Gaokao Guidance for 12.9 Million Students The 2026 gaokao season has introduced an unprecedented ...

item.title: Insta360 at a Crossroads: 37 Billion Yuan Lock-Up Expiry Collides with DJI Rivalry
item.description: Insta360 at a Crossroads: 37 Billion Yuan Lock-Up Expiry Collides with DJI Rivalry Chinese panoramic camera leader Insta360 (688775.SH) entered a critical chap...

item.title: Xiaomi Launches Battery Upgrade Service for Older Phones as Industry Shifts Toward Device Retention
item.description: Xiaomi Launches Battery Upgrade Service for Older Phones as Industry Shifts Toward Device Retention Xiaomi has rolled out a "battery upgrade" service for selec...

item.title: Network Performance Issues in Brisbane, QLD, Australia - (BNE)
item.description: Jun 12, 01:39 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 12, 01:30 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 12, 01:24 UTC Update - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue. Jun 12, 01:17 UTC Identified - Cloudflare is investigating issues...

item.title: Elevated 431 Errors
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Sora (Operational) Images (Operational) Chat Completions (Operational) Fine-tuning (Operational) Files (Operational) Moderations (Operational) Responses (Operational) Login (Operational) Batch (Operational) Audio...

item.title: Elevated number of TTFB in PHL
item.description: Jun 12, 00:45 UTC Resolved - Between 00:45 - 00:55 UTC customers reaching Philadelphia, PA may have experienced an elevated number of Time To First Byte.

item.title: Elevated error rates for GPT 5.5 in Codex
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.Affected components Codex Web (Degraded performance) CLI (Degraded performance) App (Degraded performance) VS Code extension (Degraded performance) Codex API (Degraded performance)

item.title: ShinyHunters Exploits Oracle PeopleSoft Zero-Day (CVE-2026-35273) to Breach Universities
item.description: The ShinyHunters extortion crew exploited an unpatched flaw in Oracle PeopleSoft to break into enterprise systems, steal data, and demand payment to keep it private. The campaign hit universities hardest. Google's Mandiant attributes it to the group it tracks as UNC6240, and dates the activity...

item.title: Incident with Webhooks
item.description: Jun 11, 20:00 UTC Monitoring - The degradation affecting Webhooks has been mitigated. We are monitoring to ensure stability. Jun 11, 19:58 UTC Update - We have applied a mitigation and are monitoring for recovery. Jun 11, 19:42 UTC Update - We are currently experiencing delays in Web Hook delivery...

item.title: Billing Invoice UI issue
item.description: Jun 11, 18:12 UTC Investigating - Cloudflare is investigating a Billing Dashboard UI issue. Some customers are not able to see their invoices for the last 3 months. The invoices exist in our system and automatic billing is not impacted.

item.title: Elevated errors across models
item.description: Jun 11, 17:56 UTC Resolved - The issue affecting various models has been resolved. Impact occurred from 09:42am PT / 16:42 UTC to 10:03am PT / 17:03 UTC. Jun 11, 17:27 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 16:54 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: New Attacks Trick OpenClaw AI Agent Into Running Code and Leaking Secrets
item.description: Two security teams have shown, in separate research published this week, that OpenClaw, the popular self-hosted AI agent, can be driven to run attacker-controlled code or hand over sensitive data through ordinary-looking inputs. Imperva buried instructions inside shared contacts, vCards, and...

item.title: New GreatXML Exploit Bypasses Windows BitLocker via Recovery Partition XML Files
item.description: Security researcher Chaotic Eclipse (aka Nightmare-Eclipse and MSNightmare) has released a new Windows BitLocker bypass dubbed GreatXML, a day after they published an exploit for Microsoft Defender. "This was an accidental discovery, it took a total of 4 hours to find this," the researcher said in...

item.title: The Gentlemen Ransomware Claims 478 Victims, Can Spread Like a Worm
item.description: A new analysis of The Gentlemen operation has revealed that the financially motivated threat group initially operated as an affiliate responsible for conducting double extortion attacks, while leveraging resources from various ransomware-as-a-service (RaaS) schemes like LockBit (aka Tenacious...

item.title: Workers Observability query failures
item.description: Jun 11, 16:09 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 15:52 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 15:39 UTC Identified - Cloudflare has identified an issue impacting some customers where Workers Observability queries may be returning...

item.title: Elevated errors on Claude Opus 4.6
item.description: Jun 11, 15:01 UTC Resolved - This incident has been resolved. Jun 11, 14:58 UTC Monitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results. Jun 11, 14:37 UTC Identified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented. Jun 11, 14:37 UTC Investigating - We are currently...

item.title: Disruption in service availabilty for Free and Go users
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.Affected components Conversations (Operational)

item.title: Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks
item.description: Oracle has released mitigations for CVE-2026-35273, but it has not said whether it’s a zero-day exploited in ShinyHunters attacks. The post Oracle Addresses PeopleSoft Vulnerability Amid Reports of Zero-Day Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own
item.description: As alert volumes outpace human capacity, organizations are turning to AI, automation, and deeper context to separate real threats from the noise. The post Alert Fatigue Is Becoming a Security Threat of Its Own appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Cybersecurity Stars Awards 2026: Winners Announced Across 95 Categories
item.description: Most good security work is invisible by design. Today is the exception. The 2026 Cybersecurity Stars Awards winners are announced across 95 subcategories in four main award categories. The reason is simple. Cybersecurity is full of work that deserves recognition and rarely gets it. Products that...

item.title: CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk
item.description: The new BOD 26-04 requires agencies to review and update vulnerability management policies with a focus on KEV catalog entries. The post CISA Directs Federal Agencies to Prioritize Security Patches Based on Risk appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: OnyxC2 Stealer Offers Cybercriminals Enterprise-Grade Theft for $250 a Month
item.description: Researchers say the OnyxC2 malware targets more than 200 applications and extensions while evading detection through encrypted payloads, DLL sideloading, and in-memory execution techniques. The post OnyxC2 Stealer Offers Cybercriminals Enterprise-Grade Theft for $250 a Month appeared first on...

item.title: The Download: soccer’s data renaissance and China’s big nuclear plans
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Inside soccer’s data renaissance Imagine tuning in to the opening kickoff of a World Cup match and seeing a player intentionally kick the ball out of...

item.title: Hackers Exploit Langflow Vulnerability for Remote Code Execution
item.description: Disclosed in March, the security defect enables unauthenticated attackers to write files to arbitrary locations on the system. The post Hackers Exploit Langflow Vulnerability for Remote Code Execution appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Google DeepMind is worried about what happens when millions of agents start to interact
item.description: Google DeepMind is funding research into the potential dangers of situations where millions of different AI agents interact with each other online. According to Rohin Shah, who directs the company’s AGI safety and alignment research, the mass-market arrival of agents that can carry out tasks...

item.title: Job titles of the future: Nature’s drug designer
item.description: In 2018, after nearly two decades working in Big Pharma, chemist Tim Cernak was ready to put his skills to a new use.  For Merck, he’d developed precision therapies for cancer, HIV, and diabetes that could target disease while minimizing harm to healthy cells. But as a lifelong nature lover,...

item.title: Inside soccer’s data renaissance
item.description: Imagine tuning in to the opening kickoff of a World Cup match and seeing a player intentionally send the ball all the way down the pitch and right out of bounds on the opponent’s end. Casual fans might scratch their heads. Where’s the logic in surrendering possession seconds into a game? If you...

item.title: Why China is betting on big nuclear reactors
item.description: It’s a tale of two nuclear industries. In China, large reactors are coming together at a stunning pace. The country has nearly doubled its nuclear fleet since 2016, reaching nearly 60 gigawatts of total power capacity. The new facilities are nearly all gigawatt-scale pressurized-water reactors....

item.title: Title: Network issues in Saskatoon and Calgary
item.description: Jun 11, 04:30 UTC Resolved - Cloudflare may have experienced issues with network performance in Saskatoon and Calgary between 04:20 - 04:45 UTC. The incident is now resolved

item.title: [AINews] Open Models, Model Labs vs Agent Labs, and What's Untrainable — Sarah Guo
item.description: a quiet day lets us reflect on a great essay

item.title: INC20000032
item.description: Jun 11, 00:30 UTC Resolved - Current status: We identified and reverted a misconfigured change, and monitored the environment to confirm that service was restored. If you experience additional issues or have questions, please open a support case via Snowflake Community or the Support page in...

item.title: Profiling in PyTorch (Part 2): From nn.Linear to a Fused MLP
Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
item.description: Reinforcement learning (RL) has become a key component in modern large language models, yet the rollout stage remains the key bottleneck in RL training pipelines. Although Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) offers a natural solution to accelerate rollouts through speculative decoding, many studies have...
